text:
Once upon a time in a small village nestled between rolling hills, there lived an owl named Owlia. Owlia was famous throughout the woods for her keen eyesight and remarkable vocabulary. Every morning she woke up feeling quite philosophical. One especially foggy morning she thought introspectively, "What do I see?"

She flew higher than usual, searching. She saw just a rabbit trotting away. Yes, rabbits were everywhere. She kept thinking, "What do I see?"

Flying back down she realized something wonderful. "I see beauty," she murmured softly. Owlia loved to see beauty. With those words, she turned south towards sunny spots she thought might suit her views. That afternoon she watched a flock of golden eagles hunt across valleys. They were majestic. She felt grateful.

That night Owlia dreamed she was writing poetry. Her first poem went: "In the dusk's embrace I see.”

And it was beautiful. 

* * *

That evening Owlia felt content.
